title: The Invasion Apocalypse
description: Survive the chaos of a post-apocalyptic invasion with challenging mobs, new mechanics, and a world on the brink of destruction.
body:
# The Invasion Apocalypse

It began in the cold silence of the Arctic.
As global temperatures rose, the ice began to melt — and something ancient awoke beneath it.
A microscopic organism, frozen for thousands of years, emerged from the permafrost.
At first, it was harmless. But then… it began to change.

This organism quickly adapted to its new environment, infecting the weak — animals, humans, anything alive. It consumed and replicated, reshaping its hosts into twisted, nightmarish forms.
Governments tried to contain it. They failed.
The infection spread across continents, devouring everything in its path.

Now the world is collapsing.
Nature is corrupted, cities are abandoned, and only a few survivors remain. You are one of them.

**About the Modpack**
The Invasion Apocalypse is a realistic and terrifying survival experience inspired by biological horror and parasitic evolution.
Built around Scape and Run: Parasites, the infection grows stronger the longer you survive — until no place is safe anymore.

**Features**
- Dynamic infection system — parasites evolve and adapt to your actions.
- Realistic survival — limited resources, harsh environments, and constant danger.
- Atmospheric visuals and sound — every night feels colder, every shadow more alive.
- Progressive difficulty — the world changes the longer you live.
- Supports singleplayer and multiplayer survival.


## Lore – The Beginning of the End

It all started in the Arctic, far away from civilization.
When the ice began to melt, scientists uncovered something buried deep within the frozen wastelands — an organism unlike anything known to man. At first, they believed it to be a harmless microbe, a remnant of a time long before humanity. They brought it to their laboratories, eager to study it, unaware that they had just awoken something ancient… something alive.

The organism began to move. Slowly. Silently. It adapted to the new world with terrifying speed.
It fed on oxygen, heat, and organic matter — and soon it found its first host.
A single researcher became infected. Within hours, the parasite had rewritten his body, reshaping flesh into something grotesque and unrecognizable. His screams echoed through the frozen base… and then there was silence.

Days later, the outpost stopped responding.
Rescue teams were sent — but none returned.
Satellites later captured images of the base, now covered in a strange, pulsating growth spreading across the snow. Then it vanished under a blizzard, as if nature itself was trying to hide what had been unleashed.

Months passed.
And then reports began to surface — strange creatures in remote villages, whole populations disappearing overnight, animals behaving unnaturally. The infection had found its way south. It learned, adapted, and multiplied. It no longer needed the cold — it could live anywhere.

Now, humanity is on the brink of extinction.
Cities burn, forests rot, and the sky turns gray with ash and spores. The world has become a battlefield between the last survivors and the ever-evolving parasite. It cannot be reasoned with, it cannot be stopped — it only spreads.

You are among the few still alive.
But survival has a cost — trust no one, for even the closest ally could already be infected.
The invasion has begun.
And the world as you knew it… is gone forever.
